name: Hayagriva

visual_attributes.morphology: Human body with a horse's head
visual_attributes.texture: Smooth and brilliant like fresh cut crystal
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Seated with four arms, one hand in blessing or teaching pose, others holding symbolic items

domain_specific_visual.POSE: Seated, often in meditation or blessing posture

domain_specific_functional.ROLE_IN_MYTHOLOGY: Avatar of Vishnu to recover the Vedas and defeat demons
domain_specific_functional.WORSHIP_CONTEXT: Invoked for blessings in education and wisdom

relationships.description: Hayagriva is an avatar of the god Vishnu.
Often worshipped along with his consort Lakshmi as Lakshmi-Hayagriva.
Defeated the demons Madhu and Kaitabha to recover the Vedas.

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Primarily India, influence in Southeast Asia
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents the triumph of knowledge over ignorance and chaos

contrastive_constraints: NOT depicted with a fearsome or demonic expression
NOT associated with non-Hindu contexts or deities
